meta.method: manual source audit (call-site attribution); NOT auto-generated. See README.md.
meta.purpose: Map an egress hostname to the opencode subsystem that dials it, so the network prompt can render a human-readable 'likely cause' line. The proxy never terminates TLS, so only host+port+source-process are observable; this map resolves the cause from the hostname alone.

origin_values.opencode-core: Dialed by the opencode server/bun runtime itself (background). Not separable from agent LLM traffic by PID — same process.
origin_values.opencode-tool: Dialed by a subprocess opencode spawns for language tooling (LSP server, ripgrep). May appear as a distinct PID.
origin_values.provider: LLM inference endpoint; only dialed when that provider is configured.
origin_values.external: Observed at runtime but NOT attributable to opencode source (dependency/runtime). Out of scope for this map.

entries.cause: Syntax-highlighting grammar/query (tree-sitter) for the code you are viewing
entries.notes: Also used by the windows scoop self-update check (installation/index.ts). If the source process is an agent tool rather than opencode itself, treat as an agent-initiated fetch instead.

entries.cause: Asset download: tree-sitter grammar (.wasm), an LSP server, the ripgrep binary, or a self-update
GitHub release-asset CDN (redirect target of a github.com release download)
entries.source: (redirect target; not a literal in source)

entries.cause: GitHub source-archive download for an LSP (e.g. vscode-eslint, elixir-ls)
GitHub API: latest-release lookup for an LSP server (kotlin, lua, texlab, tinymist, zls), opencode self-update, or GitHub app integration
npm registry: installing an LSP server (typescript, pyright, ...) or a plugin
entries.notes: Default registry; overridable via .npmrc `registry`. A custom registry host serves the same purpose.

entries.cause: opencode config schema, TUI manifest, install script, docs, changelog, or the Zen LLM gateway (/zen/v1)
opencode auth (device-code login) and Zen gateway connection setup

entries.notes: Representative of the provider class. Any configured provider host (openai, x.ai, openrouter, bedrock, vertex/googleapis, a self-hosted gateway, etc.) is category llm-inference. Cannot be separated from opencode-core background traffic by PID — same process.

opt_in.notes: OPT-IN ONLY: no export unless OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set. A Datadog intake host seen at runtime comes from local/TNG env config, not an opencode default.

not_egress.note: NOT an opencode egress. Appears only as source text — a webfetch test fixture (packages/core/test/tool-webfetch.test.ts, a dummy 'Unable to fetch' target) and a code comment (packages/opencode/src/permission/arity.ts:96). Never dialed at runtime (absent from the sandbox audit). Documented so it is not re-investigated.
